steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage establishes that resistance develops when repellents activate multiple receptors, but Ke Dong's breakthrough is that EBF repels mosquitoes by activating just one receptor—Or31—which is present in all disease-carrying species. The “Therefore” signals that the next step should extend this single-receptor strategy.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: The most useful approach for developing new repellents is to identify other molecular components that also target Or31, giving researchers multiple options based on this successful single-receptor pattern. The other three choices pursue extraction methods, receptor location mapping, or activating multiple receptors—none of which follow from discovering that single-receptor activation solves the resistance problem. That matches choice A — A is correct.
